title: Macramé pillow cover
subtitle: A knotted front panel on a linen or cotton backing, fitted to a 45 cm cushion pad
excerpt: Work a textured macramé front panel in 4 mm natural cotton cord, then stitch it onto a linen or cotton backing fabric with an envelope back opening. The finished cover fits a standard 45 cm square cushion pad.
sourceNotes: Synthesised from contemporary British macramé practice. Knotted cushion covers with fabric backs are a current guild project; the panel-on-backing construction is the standard format for structured macramé cushions.

glossaryTerms.definition: The core macramé knot: two working cords tied around two filler cords, first left over right, then right over left.
A simple knotting movement in which one cord is looped once around another. Double half hitches, worked in diagonal or horizontal rows, create solid lines or shapes in macramé.
A mounting knot for attaching folded cords to a dowel or horizontal cord. Used here to mount all the working cords at the start of the panel.

content.text: A macramé pillow cover is worked as a flat panel that is then stitched onto a backing fabric. The panel is built on a horizontal dowel using
rows for texture. The backing fabric (linen or heavy cotton) provides stability and allows the cushion to zip or button.

items.name: Linen or heavy cotton backing fabric, natural colour
items.qty: 70 cm x 50 cm (for front + envelope back)

items.name: Strong thread and needle for sewing the panel to the backing

content.text: Cut 24 lengths of cord, each 180 cm long. Fold each in half and mount on the dowel using lark's head knots, giving 48 working cords. Space the knots about 1 cm apart. Pin the dowel to the macramé board horizontally.

Work 5 rows of alternating square knots to establish the ground structure. Then work 3 rows of diagonal double half hitches from left to right across the full width, using the leftmost cord as the holding cord. Follow with 5 more rows of alternating square knots, then 3 rows of diagonal double half hitches from right to left. Repeat this sequence (5 rows square knots, 3 rows diagonal half hitches) until the panel is 45 cm long.
Work one final row of square knots across all cords. Trim the cord ends to 3 cm below the last knot and apply a drop of fabric glue to each end to prevent fraying.

Cut two pieces of backing fabric for the envelope back: each 45 cm x 30 cm, with a hemmed inner edge. Lay the macramé panel face-up on the outer backing fabric piece (45 cm x 45 cm). Stitch the panel to the backing around all four edges using a strong running stitch, holding the macramé panel flat against the backing. Then stitch the two envelope back pieces to the assembled front, right sides together, and turn through. Insert the cushion pad through the envelope opening.

items.problem: The macramé panel is smaller than the backing fabric
items.cause: The square knot rows drew in as knotting progressed
items.fix: Use T-pins at the selvedge edges as you work to maintain the correct width. The panel should measure at least 45 cm across at every stage.

items.problem: The half-hitch diagonal rows are not straight
items.cause: The holding cord was not kept taut during knotting
items.fix: Pin the holding cord to the macramé board at the holding angle before beginning each diagonal row. The holding cord must stay straight for the row to be consistent.
